events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess hazard: neutrals & grounds mixed in subpanel. In subpanel (separately derived system), neutral and ground must be separated; neutral bus isolated from enclosure, ground bus bonded to enclosure. Mixing neutrals and grounds on same bus in subpanel causes objectionable current on grounding paths, shock hazard, violates NEC 408.3? Also 200.4? Need identify safety hazards severity immediate controls. Decision fail because dangerous/non-compliant. Risk high? Could be high due to shock/electrocution, ground fault current path issues. Work stage in-progress. Component conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete maybe 70? Value not asked 0. Sound etc default. Findings: neutrals and equipment grounding conductors terminated on common bus in subpanel; neutral bus not isolated from enclosure; EGCs carrying normal neutral current; potential shock hazard on metallic enclosures; ground fault protection compromised. Actions: de-energize panel, separate neutrals and grounds, install isolated neutral bus and ground bus bonded to enclosure, verify proper termination torque, test continuity/isolation, reinspect before energization. Rationale. References NEC Art. 408 (panelboards), 200.4 (neutral conductor), 250 maybe? But only given references. Also mention 110.26 if working clearance not observed? Scenario only mentions neutrals & grounds mixed. Could mention 408.3, 200.4. Need include 110.26? Maybe not directly applicable but reference material includes it. We can include as relevant for working clearance during correction. But findings focus on mixing. Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside.

events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 65,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Neutral conductors and equipment grounding conductors are terminated on the same bus in a subpanel.",
    "Neutral bus is not isolated from the subpanel enclosure, creating a parallel path for normal neutral current on grounding conductors.",
    "Metallic enclosures and downstream equipment may become energized with objectionable current, presenting shock/electrocution risk.",
    "Ground-fault current path integrity is degraded, reducing effectiveness of overcurrent protection during a fault.",
    "Condition violates NEC requirements for separation of grounded and grounding conductors in panelboards."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "De-energize the subpanel and verify it is electrically safe before correction.",
    "Remove all neutral conductors from the grounding bus and terminate them on a properly insulated/isolated neutral bus.",
    "Terminate all equipment grounding conductors on a ground bus that is solidly bonded to the subpanel enclosure.",
    "Confirm the neutral bus is electrically isolated from the enclosure and the ground bus is bonded to it.",
    "Check and re-torque all terminations to manufacturer specifications.",
    "Perform continuity and isolation testing before re-energizing.",
    "Re-inspect and obtain approval before returning the panel to service."
  ],
  "rationale": "Neutrals and grounds mixed in a subpanel create objectionable current on grounding paths, shock hazards, and impaired ground-fault protection. This is a code violation and an unsafe condition that must be corrected before the panel is energized or accepted.",
  "references": [
    "NEC Art. 408",
    "NEC 200.4",
    "NEC 110.26"
  ]
}
